parsed_review.summary: CP00-488 is a clean descriptor-only governance-core slice (RP16.P02.M07.S13-RP16.P02.M09.S08, risk B, 40 units) and its production-readiness posture is correctly bounded: the pack does NOT claim production_ready or enterprise trust. Verified end-to-end consistency: pack identity, risk class, unit count (40), range, next_pack_id (CP00-489), deliverable distribution (impl 16/claude_review 2/ui 4/failure_recovery 4/test 8/contract 2/security_audit 4), and micro-title distribution (Test/Golden 10, Hermes Evidence 22, Claude Review 8) match across baseline, registry binding, service/validator code, contract current_pack + p02_test_hermes_slice_descriptor, and the live closeout-pack-plan.json. Boundaries hold: every CP488 result is frozen with writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, all dispatches_*_runtime=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, creates/updates_database_rows=false, loads_real_fixture_data=false, leak/permission_bypass=false; a source sweep of packages/governance/src found no real I/O, network, process, or SQL (only internal imports and *:false flags). Claude is held to read-only independent reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true). Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (receipt_shape ...no_write), not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is fully configured: invalid_review_blockers cover auth failure, zero-byte, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/inspection-basis, final-approval claim, source-mutation, and enterprise-trust claim; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON; validation-report confirms prompt size, no full-diff/source, summary-only prohibited, no forbidden tools. The validator's strict deepEqual between contract and code-generated descriptor plus pinned current_pack/no_write_attestation would catch any drift. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are correctly absent at this pre-adjudication stage. Command-evidence reports all required validation commands at exit 0 (claimed; not independently re-run under read-only constraints, but structurally corroborated). No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
